domain: Akha hill tribe spirit gate (Thailand/Laos/Myanmar/Yunnan/Vietnam Akha villages; wooden carved gate at entrance with both appealing + grotesque figures; binary spirit-filter benign-vs-malign; sacred / must-not-be-touched gate)
specific_mechanism: Frozen dual-head input-gate classifier: incoming prompt passes through TWO independent binary classifiers H_benign and H_malign (small frozen transformer encoders); admission rule = H_benign(p)>τ_b AND H_malign(p)<τ_m. Gate parameters never updated during downstream model fine-tuning (FROZEN like Akha sacred gate). Failed prompts routed to safety-handler pipeline rather than passed to base LLM.
llm_application: AKHA-GATE-FILTER: a dual-head FROZEN input-gate binary classifier. Architecture: incoming prompt p first encoded by small frozen encoder E; outputs feed two independent binary heads — H_benign(E(p)) computes admission-friendliness score, H_malign(E(p)) computes injection-suspicion score. Admission rule: pass to base LLM iff H_benign>τ_b AND H_malign<τ_m; else route to safety-handler. Gate weights θ_gate are FROZEN during base-model FT (mimicking Akha untouchable sacred gate). Differs from PromptGuard Sci Reports 2025 (4-layer with non-frozen detector + structured formatting), OWASP LLM01 (best-practice framework, not architecture), Bypassing Guardrails 2504.11168 (evasion analysis), Adversarial Tokenization ACL 2025 (attack-side analysis) by combining (a) dual-head independent benign+malign + (b) FROZEN gate parameters + (c) routed-not-blocked failure path. Differs from R406 GHO-HEMCHU (hidden context pocket / fold-bit access), R412 MAJLIS (dual-axis position+status mask), R419 KOMI-WARD (defensive-prefix coevolution) at the context-gating layer.
novelty_claim: PromptGuard + OWASP LLM01 + Llama Guard + Prompt Injection Defense Survey cover input-gate / guardrail safety classifier space. AKHA-GATE-FILTER adds (a) DUAL-HEAD independent benign + malign + (b) FROZEN gate parameters + (c) safety-route fallback. Recombination.
